basics.name: Jérémie Jost
basics.label: Engineering Manager · Backend & Data Engineering
basics.summary: Engineering Manager with 15+ years of software engineering experience, including 9 years at Zalando across individual contributor, tech lead, and management roles. Brings hands-on depth in backend services, cloud infrastructure, and data engineering to a leadership style focused on lean delivery, high autonomy, and ownership. Proven track record of growing engineers, shipping high-stakes initiatives, and raising team engagement well above organisational benchmarks.

work.name: Zalando
work.summary: Leading Team Express, a backend & data engineering team of 4, within Zalando's mission-critical e-commerce platform. Full management scope covering people, delivery, architecture, and operational excellence.
highlights: Defined Service Level Objectives (SLOs) and oversaw incident response and post-mortem processes to maintain production reliability.
Owned the team's architectural roadmap and quality standards, ensuring alignment with company-wide engineering principles.
Led performance and compensation cycles end-to-end, securing merit-based outcomes for high performers through structured calibration.
Achieved top-tier scores in company-wide team engagement surveys across both support and value dimensions, consistently above organisational average.

work.name: Zalando
work.summary: Bridged technical execution and business strategy, evolving the team from reactive delivery into a proactive, outcome-driven organisation with clear prioritisation and stakeholder visibility.
highlights: Led a high-stakes backend pivot across three product workstreams with a mixed team of 6 (3 core, 3 contractors), delivering on time despite 50% capacity reduction in supporting areas.
Managed end-to-end delivery of a global trend-discovery feature rollout across major fashion capitals, then successfully transitioned ownership to a partner department.
Introduced a rolling weekly planning framework with explicit WIP limits, eliminating reactive firefighting and giving stakeholders real-time visibility into team priorities.
Built a self-serve analytics dashboard that removed a recurring engineering bottleneck, enabling product partners to manage configurations independently.
Ran full-cycle hiring for a Senior Engineer role; defined job specs and interview scorecards, extended a successful offer, and overhauled contractor onboarding.

work.name: Zalando
work.summary: Technical anchor for the team, driving reliability, cross-team alignment, and production readiness at scale.
highlights: Coordinated platform-wide risk reviews for Cyber Week (2021 & 2022), identifying and mitigating critical issues across core pillars — zero incidents during the company's highest-traffic events.

Architected the navigation infrastructure for the personalised browse experience: designed distributed tracing, defined SLO/SLIs, and built load tests that accurately predicted peak traffic behaviour.
Resolved cross-team data quality issues in a shared data pipeline to unblock A/B test integrity across personalised carousels.
Mentored an engineer through a structured development plan, resulting in a successful promotion to Senior level.

work.name: Zalando
work.summary: Early engineer in Zalando's newly established Dublin office, building the social media crawling infrastructure and REST API that surfaced fashion influencer content across the organisation — work that ultimately powered Collabary, Zalando's brand-influencer marketing platform.
highlights: Built a social media crawler (Scala, Kafka, Postgres, AWS) indexing thousands of fashion influencers across Instagram and YouTube.
Designed and shipped a REST API exposing influencer content for internal consumption, becoming a foundational data source for Collabary.
One of the first engineers to take a live service to production in the newly opened Zalando Dublin office.
Later pivoted to building a more generic web crawler for competitor price-matching use cases.

work.name: Waratek
work.summary: Core engineer on a multi-tenant JVM platform, improving runtime tooling, release reliability, and developer workflow across the VM codebase.
highlights: Maintained and tested critical JVM backend components, including host-to-guest APIs, serialization, and JMX integration (Java).
Built and supported CLI tooling for container creation and lifecycle management, improving operability for JVM users (Python, Scala, JMX).
Improved engineering throughput by extending Mercurial workflows for pull-request handling and by deploying static analysis checks in code review (Python).
Strengthened release discipline by introducing Ivy-based dependency management, semantic versioning, and shorter fixed release cycles.

work.name: Civitec
work.summary: Contributed to Pro-SiVIC sensor simulation releases while helping evolve the product from research prototype toward an industry-ready platform.
highlights: Ported the UI stack from .NET/WinForms to Qt4, modernizing the frontend and improving maintainability.
Enhanced the simulation core with a reusable property system and generic object serialization to enable richer UI-driven configuration.
Introduced engineering execution practices including bug tracking, project planning, unit testing, and daily builds.
Improved team alignment and delivery cadence by instituting recurring status reporting and weekly coordination rituals.

work.name: Voxelia
work.summary: Early engineer across internships and freelance engagements, helping a young startup deliver initial customer-facing simulation and visualization products.
highlights: Led development of a technical demo showcasing urban environment modeling and traffic/pedestrian simulation capabilities (Unity3D, C#).
Built 3D visualization software for the Belfort-Montbéliard urban area, supporting real-world geographic rendering use cases (C++, Qt, OpenSceneGraph).
Prototyped a scenario editor and contributed to road traffic simulation components for the in-house simulation platform (C++, Qt, Java).
